text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: Gods, grant me victory in the Arima<n>Kinen!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
...Is what I used to say, but not today.<n>Today, I'm
going to make it on my<n>own.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
This time, I want the gods to sit back<n>and watch.
I'll be doing all the work.<n>So my wish instead will
be...

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: For... Uh? Uhhhh...

What do I do, Trainer?! I don't have<n>anything to
ask for!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: The diva of divination has run out of<n>prayers!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Nothing is coming to mind. I've<n>already got good
fortune, and I know<n>exactly what I want for
myself!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Have I lost my mojo? Could it be my<n>well of faith
has run dry?!
choice_data_list: Couldn't it just mean that you're satisfied?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: Satisfied? I suppose I do feel<n>somewhat content...

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Oh, that solves it! There's no need for<n>wishes
when you're this happy.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Hm... While this is a nice feeling at<n>first, I say it's a
cause of concern.<n>What will the gods think of me if
I<n>show up with nothing to say?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: If I just had even one little thing to<n>ask for...

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
We're at the shrine, but<n>Matikanefukukitaru can't
think of<n>anything to pray for.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
No... I can't go out there like this! To<n>think I can no
longer perform my rites<n>when it really counts.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Nothing can beat this overwhelming<n>happiness I
feel. So what can I pray<n>for other than victory...
choice_data_list: What about praying for everyone's happiness?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: Everyone's... happiness? Not my<n>own?

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
I remind her that all the people who<n>cheered her
on are a big part of why<n>she has been able to
come so far.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
That's true. I'm only happy thanks to<n>everyone's
support. It's about time I<n>return the favor.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Now that my dream has come true,<n>it's my turn to
make everyone else<n>happy!

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Her expression is of unabashed<n>dignity—perhaps
the heavens truly do<n>smile upon her.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Would I make everyone happy if I<n>won the Arima
Kinen?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Okay. Then praying might not be<n>what's needed
here.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
You see, praying isn't the only thing<n>you can do
before the gods.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
I'm still learning the ropes, but<n>today's the perfect
day to give this a<n>real shot.

Hark! O Shiraoki, who resides on this<n>sanctified
altar, heed my oath!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: To bring joy to everyone who taughtme to stand proud, I will win theArima Kinen!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: I humbly make this vow before all the<n>gods!

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Her voice rings out clearly through the<n>cold
December skies, delivering her<n>solemn promise.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text: The Arima Kinen will soon be upon us.
